app.name: ElevenLabs
app.bestFor: The default for the most human-sounding TTS, best voice cloning, 70+ languages and voice agents.
app.rankBasis: The most consistently recommended premium AI voice platform across 2026 TTS roundups, with a large creator/developer user base and especially strong independent recognition for natural speech and voice cloning.
app.pricingPaid: Starter $5/mo — 30,000 credits/mo, commercial license and instant voice cloning
features: sound effects, dubbing and music generation
pros: reviewers consistently rate its speech naturalness and emotional range among the best
broad API and creator-tool ecosystem
strong multilingual voice-cloning quality
cons: high-quality models consume credits quickly
usage costs rise sharply for long-form or production-scale audio
voice-cloning consent and rights require careful governance

app.name: Suno
app.bestFor: The quality benchmark for AI music generation (full songs with vocals). Suno v5 leads audio-fidelity ELO.
app.rankBasis: A top-two pick in current AI-music comparisons and the best-known full-song generator, with unusually broad mainstream adoption and benchmark leadership for prompt-to-song output with vocals.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: 50 credits/day (about 10 songs), non-commercial use
app.pricingPaid: Pro $10/mo — 2,500 credits/mo, commercial-use rights and priority generation
pros: fastest path from a short prompt to a complete, polished song
strong vocals and genre coverage
generous daily free allowance for experimentation
cons: fine-grained composition and arrangement control remains limited
results can sound formulaic across repeated generations
free-plan songs cannot be used commercially

app.name: Murf
app.bestFor: Studio-style TTS for voiceovers, e-learning and presentations. Reddit's top workhorse pick.
app.rankBasis: A recurring top enterprise/creator TTS recommendation in major roundups, backed by a substantial G2/Capterra review footprint and particularly strong mindshare for presentation and e-learning voiceovers.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: 10 minutes of voice generation; no downloads
app.pricingPaid: Creator from $19/mo billed annually — 2 projects and 24 hours of voice generation/year
pros: reviewers praise the approachable studio workflow
good fit for business voiceovers and training content
useful collaboration and pronunciation controls
cons: free output cannot be downloaded
less convincing for highly emotional performance than specialist voice tools
plan limits and annual usage accounting can be confusing

app.name: Speechify
app.bestFor: Best for listening — turn articles, docs and books into natural speech across devices.
app.rankBasis: One of the most widely installed consumer text-to-speech readers and a frequent top pick for accessibility and document listening, with far broader end-user awareness than most creator-focused TTS tools.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: basic voices and standard reading speeds across web and mobile
app.pricingPaid: Premium $29/mo — 200+ natural voices, 60+ languages, higher speeds and offline listening
features: read web pages, PDFs and documents aloud
pros: strong cross-device reading experience
especially useful for accessibility, study and long documents
simple browser and document ingestion
cons: premium subscription is expensive for a reading utility
best voices and offline features are paywalled
not a full production voiceover studio

app.name: Udio
app.bestFor: Music generation rival to Suno, favored by some for vocal texture and remixing.
app.rankBasis: Regularly compared directly with Suno as the other leading consumer AI-music generator; independent creator discussions often favor its vocal texture, editing workflow and remixability despite lower overall adoption.
app.pricingFree: Free plan: 10 daily credits plus a 100-credit monthly pool
pros: creators often prefer its vocal texture and musical detail
more useful editing and remix controls than simple prompt-only tools
free tier supports meaningful evaluation
cons: generation can be slower and less predictable than Suno
complete-song coherence varies across extensions
commercial and upload rights depend on plan and source ownership

app.name: Fish Audio
app.bestFor: Closest open/low-cost alternative to ElevenLabs quality for cloning and TTS.
app.rankBasis: A fast-growing lower-cost ElevenLabs alternative with a heavily starred open model behind it, but still a thinner independent-review footprint than the category leaders.
app.pricingFree: Free tier available with limited monthly TTS and voice-cloning usage
app.pricingPaid: Paid credit packs and subscription tiers available; public allowances vary by model
pros: strong quality-to-cost ratio in community comparisons
fast cloning with broad language support
open-model ecosystem reduces vendor lock-in
cons: smaller independent review base than ElevenLabs or Murf
voice quality varies across community models
pricing and model-specific credit consumption are not always easy to compare

skill.name: Coqui TTS (XTTS)
skill.bestFor: The gold-standard open-source TTS / voice-cloning toolkit (~45.8k GitHub★) — XTTS v2 clones a voice from a 3-second clip across many languages.
skill.rankBasis: One of the most-starred open-source TTS toolkits at roughly 46k GitHub stars and a standard recommendation in open voice-cloning roundups, though the original repository is now archived.
pros: mature toolkit with broad model and language coverage
high-quality zero-shot cloning from a short reference clip
large body of community examples and integrations
cons: upstream repository is archived and no longer actively maintained
GPU setup and model compatibility can be difficult
license terms differ across included models and must be checked

skill.name: Bark (Suno)
skill.bestFor: Suno's transformer TTS (~39.2k GitHub★) — highly expressive speech plus non-speech sounds (laughter, sighs, music). A recognized open leader.
skill.rankBasis: About 39k GitHub stars and still one of the best-known expressive open speech generators, repeatedly included in local-TTS lists for producing speech, laughter, music and other audio from one model.
features: speech plus laughter, sighs and music
pros: unusually expressive output beyond conventional TTS
simple experimentation with pretrained checkpoints
large community and extensive derivative tooling
cons: slow and memory-heavy compared with newer TTS models
limited deterministic control over timing and wording
voice cloning and commercial usage are not its strongest path

sources.name: Local AI Master best local TTS

skill.name: OpenVoice (MyShell)
skill.bestFor: MyShell's instant voice cloning (~37k GitHub★) — strong tone/emotion control and low-latency inference. Widely adopted open cloner.
skill.rankBasis: Roughly 37k GitHub stars and broad citation in open voice-cloning comparisons make it one of the most adopted instant-cloning projects, especially for cross-lingual tone and style transfer.
pros: clones a speaker from a short reference sample
strong style control across languages and accents
lighter inference path than many training-based cloners
cons: setup spans multiple checkpoints and dependencies
output quality depends heavily on the base TTS model
less turnkey than a hosted voice API

skill.name: RVC (Retrieval-based Voice Conversion)
skill.bestFor: The de-facto open voice-conversion tool (~36.6k GitHub★) — best balance of quality, speed and ease for real-time voice changing/cloning.
skill.rankBasis: The de-facto open voice-conversion WebUI at roughly 37k GitHub stars, with the largest hobbyist model-sharing ecosystem and frequent community recommendations for real-time singing and speech conversion.
pros: strong quality and speed for voice-to-voice conversion
large community model ecosystem
accessible WebUI for training and inference
cons: not text-to-speech; it requires a source performance
CUDA, audio-driver and model setup can be fragile
community voice models can create serious consent and copyright risks

skill.name: Fish Speech (fishaudio)
skill.bestFor: Fast, high-quality multilingual TTS / cloning (~31.4k GitHub★) — a newer entry gaining strong traction for its speed-quality balance.
skill.rankBasis: Around 31k GitHub stars and rapid adoption in local-TTS communities place it among the leading newer multilingual speech models, with notable recognition for its speed-to-quality balance.
pros: strong naturalness and multilingual cloning quality
efficient inference relative to many large speech models
active development and a growing ecosystem
cons: hardware requirements remain substantial for best performance
installation and checkpoint choices can confuse new users
model licensing is more restrictive than permissive OSS code alone suggests

sources.name: Local AI Master best local TTS

skill.name: Chatterbox (Resemble AI)
skill.bestFor: Resemble AI's open TTS (~25.7k GitHub★) — a top 2026 pick for expressive, production-grade local speech with emotion control.
skill.rankBasis: A breakout open TTS project at roughly 26k GitHub stars and a recurring 2026 recommendation for expressive local speech, backed by an established commercial voice vendor.
pros: expressive output with direct emotion controls
production-oriented project from an experienced voice vendor
good cloning quality from short references
cons: newer project with less long-term operational evidence
GPU inference and model downloads are substantial
watermarking and model-license constraints may affect some workflows

skill.name: F5-TTS
skill.bestFor: A fast diffusion-transformer TTS with strong zero-shot cloning (~15k GitHub★) — notable and actively used, below the top repos on adoption.
skill.rankBasis: Approximately 15k GitHub stars and strong research/community visibility make it a notable zero-shot TTS implementation, though adoption and production tooling remain below the larger open projects.
pros: high-quality cloning with natural prosody
fast architecture relative to older diffusion TTS
useful research code and reproducible recipes
cons: more research-oriented than turnkey
checkpoint and dependency setup requires ML experience
commercial rights depend on the selected model and training data

skill.name: ElevenLabs Skills (official)
skill.bestFor: Official ElevenLabs Agent Skills bundle — TTS, music, sound effects, voice changer and isolator. Vendor-official but low adoption (~400★) vs the OSS models.
skill.rankBasis: The vendor-official ElevenLabs Agent Skills bundle, but only about 400 GitHub stars; its authority and breadth justify inclusion while adoption remains far below the open speech-model leaders.
skill.pricingFree: Free skill code; ElevenLabs API free tier provides 10,000 credits/mo
skill.pricingPaid: ElevenLabs Starter $5/mo — 30,000 credits/mo and commercial license
pros: officially maintained examples for the vendor API
covers several audio tasks in one skill bundle
quick path to high-quality managed speech
cons: requires an ElevenLabs account and API key
inherits API credit costs and rate limits
small independent user base compared with the underlying service

skill.name: elevenlabs-voices (robbyczgw-cla)
skill.bestFor: Ready-made voice personas/presets (18 voices) over the ElevenLabs API for quick, consistent TTS from an agent. Thin/single-author.
skill.rankBasis: A thin single-author skill with 18 ready-made ElevenLabs personas and little measurable GitHub adoption; useful as a preset library but not independently validated at scale.
skill.pricingFree: Free skill code; ElevenLabs API free tier provides 10,000 credits/mo
skill.pricingPaid: ElevenLabs Starter $5/mo — 30,000 credits/mo and commercial license
pros: reduces prompt and voice-selection setup
useful for repeatable character voices
simple, inspectable skill implementation
cons: minimal community adoption and independent evidence
depends entirely on the ElevenLabs API
preset coverage is narrow compared with the full voice catalog

notes: Rebuilt to rank by real reputation/adoption, not form factor (kind is a metadata tag only). The category is dominated by heavyweight open-source models — Coqui TTS (~46k★), Bark, OpenVoice, RVC, Fish Speech and Chatterbox are the recognized leaders and correctly outrank the ElevenLabs official skill (~400★) and thinner single-author skills. Previous aggregator MCP entries (fal, mcp.film) and the vendor LuvVoice listing were removed. Music-first generation (Suno/Udio) still has no comparable dedicated open skill/repo, so it stays in the app track.
